page: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The system provides 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression tests place in the bottom 25 percent, while multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and PassMark extended instruction tests score in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block, read-only large block, and write large block operations all ranking in the top 25 percent. In application-level tests, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations at 74.41 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 43.63 GB/sec, while LLM text generation speeds are poor, falling in the bottom 10 percent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.24xlarge offers a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage on a dedicated hardware class. The inclusion of 3.6 TB of local NVMe SSDs eliminates the need for external block storage for high-throughput local caching. This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads such as multi-threaded database operations, high-throughput static web serving, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio alongside fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving, though single-core performance and LLM text generation are relatively weak. This instance provides a balanced resource configuration with high-speed local storage, making it highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring high-throughput local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores, high memory bandwidth, and three point six terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
3600 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core CPU and high-speed memory bandwidth benchmarks
Excellent throughput for Redis caching and static web serving
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
